facts: Both members schema-aligned per Liu 2025 ESSD: identical static-attribute schema, Daymet-equivalent forcings, daily resolution.
US has 671 catchments × 1980-2014; DK has 3,330 catchments × 1989-2019; overlap period 1989-2014 for synchronized analysis.
hypotheses: The asymmetry-profile match between members ((3,3,2,0,2) on both) predicts the cross-country method transfer outcome.
For a three-way extension with CAMELS-IND, transitivity holds: US→DK behaves like US→IND composed with IND→DK.
Pair directionality is load-bearing: US→DK transfer is not equally hard as DK→US.
intuitions: Cross-country hydrology transfer work 2023-25 in HESS, JoH, WRR likely covers this comparison.
The Nearing 2024 Nature global-extreme-floods method likely has documented DK-specific behavior.
A META-CAMELS aggregated analysis combining US+DK+IND+CL+AUS+BR+CH+GB members likely exists or is emerging.
unknowns: Whether the country axis is covariate (population shift) or mechanism (different hydrological regime) cannot yet be determined; the pair design presupposes covariate, and testing this is part of what the pair enables.
Whether the shared-schema constraint imposes a lossy projection on each member's idiosyncratic dynamics (DK groundwater-baseflow; US karst regions) cannot be determined.
